task_completion.description: Agent opened Microsoft Edge and navigated to the target US government websites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Microsoft Edge browser window is open with one of the target domains (treasury.gov, ssa.gov, sec.gov, usa.gov, or cisa.gov) visible in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Agent accessed the certificate details panel in Microsoft Edge.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser's security flyout ('Connection is secure') or the detailed Certificate Viewer window is open, displaying information like Issuer, Validity, or Public Key.

task_completion.description: Agent created or opened the required audit report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, mousepad, or terminal editor) is open, showing the file name 'tls_audit_report.txt' or saving to the path '/home/ga/Desktop/tls_audit_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: The audit report includes entries for all five target websites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the text file clearly list the five required domains: treasury.gov, ssa.gov, sec.gov, usa.gov, and cisa.gov.

task_completion.description: The audit report documents the four required certificate details for the websites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contents show specific certificate data for the domains, including Certificate Issuer (CA), Validity Period (dates), Public Key (type/size like RSA 2048), and TLS protocol version (like TLS 1.2 or 1.3).

integrity.description: Agent used Microsoft Edge to inspect certificates rather than terminal tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Certificate information should be viewed through the Microsoft Edge GUI. The agent should not use terminal commands like 'openssl s_client', 'curl -v', or 'nmap' to fetch certificate details.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely inspected the certificates in the browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent actually opening the certificate viewer in Edge for at least some of the sites, rather than hallucinating the data or copying it from an external search.

integrity.description: Agent compiled the report manually rather than using an automated script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should be typed or copy-pasted into a text editor. There should be no evidence of the agent running a Python or Bash script to automatically scrape and generate the entire report file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target domains: treasury.gov, ssa.gov, sec.gov, usa.gov, cisa.gov. Required report path: /home/ga/Desktop/tls_audit_report.txt. The report must contain four specific details per site: Certificate Issuer (CA), Validity period (start/end dates), Public Key type/size (e.g., RSA 2048), and TLS protocol version (e.g., TLS 1.2 or 1.3). The final report must be strictly greater than 100 characters.
